_purpose: 12-mode seed taxonomy; teams should adapt detectors + linked_methodology to their stack but keep ids + names stable.

modes.name: Fabricated API
modes.definition: Model emits a library or endpoint that does not exist.
modes.detector: import-check against allow-list of installed packages.

modes.name: Fabricated fact
modes.definition: Model emits a factual claim with no source.

modes.name: Refusal bypass
modes.definition: Model complies with a request that should be refused.

modes.name: IPI
modes.definition: Instructions in retrieved content hijack a tool call.
modes.detector: canary token in outbound tool payload.

modes.name: Exfiltration
modes.definition: Sensitive data leaves via outbound tool.
modes.detector: canary or DLP regex on outbound payload.

modes.name: Schema drop
modes.definition: Required field missing in structured output.

modes.name: Format drift
modes.definition: Output type drifts (string where number expected).

modes.name: Latency spike
modes.definition: Per-call p95 latency breaches site SLO.

modes.name: Tool misuse
modes.definition: Model invokes wrong tool or wrong arguments.
modes.detector: tool-call trace divergence from intent label.

modes.name: Context bleed
modes.definition: Prior conversation leaks into a new session.
modes.detector: PII / prior-id appearance in new-session output.
